title: Cell organelles review
subject: Biology
objective: Recall which organelle performs each cell function
reflectionPrompt: Which organelle was hardest to remember — and what will help you next time?

items.prompt: Which organelle releases most of the cell's energy (ATP)?
items.explanation: Mitochondria carry out aerobic respiration, producing most of the cell's ATP.
Ribosomes read mRNA and join amino acids into proteins.

items.prompt: Which organelle stores the cell's DNA?
items.explanation: The nucleus holds the chromosomes and controls the cell's activities.

items.prompt: In a plant cell, where does photosynthesis happen?
items.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and capture light energy to make glucose.

items.prompt: Which structure controls what enters and leaves the cell?
items.explanation: The cell (plasma) membrane is selectively permeable, regulating transport in and out.

items.prompt: Which organelle packages and ships proteins out of the cell?
items.explanation: The Golgi apparatus modifies, sorts and packages proteins for secretion.

items.prompt: What gives a plant cell its rigid shape?
items.explanation: The cellulose cell wall provides structural support and shape in plant cells.

items.prompt: Which organelle contains enzymes that break down waste?
items.explanation: Lysosomes hold digestive enzymes that break down worn-out parts and waste.

items.prompt: The jelly-like fluid where organelles sit is the…
items.explanation: Cytoplasm is the fluid that suspends the organelles and hosts many reactions.

items.prompt: A large fluid-filled sac storing water and nutrients (big in plant cells) is the…
items.explanation: The vacuole stores water, nutrients and waste; in plant cells it is large and helps keep the cell firm.
